parentQuestion: Following a stroke, a 62-year-old patient can understand spoken language perfect...

scaffolds.q: Language processing involves multiple specialized brain regions working together.
scaffolds.explain: Language is a distributed function requiring multiple cortical areas: Wernicke's area for comprehension, Broca's area for production, arcuate fasciculus connecting them, motor cortex for articulation, auditory cortex for sound processing. Damage to different components creates different aphasia patterns.

scaffolds.q: Broca's area is located in the frontal lobe.
scaffolds.explain: Broca's area occupies the inferior frontal gyrus (frontal lobe) adjacent to the motor cortex regions controlling facial muscles, tongue, and larynx. Its frontal location reflects its role in motor speech production.
